claim: A meta-harness sits above multiple agent harnesses, allowing all agents to share one session, history, and set of policies rather than existing in isolated boxes
actionable: Build or adopt a meta-harness layer that standardizes interfaces across Claude Code, Codex, and other harnesses to eliminate manual context copying
source_title: This Meta-Harness Changes How You Run AI Agents

claim: OmniGent enables cross-vendor code review where Claude writes and Codex reviews (or vice versa), preventing internal bias from the same agent reviewing its own code
actionable: Implement cross-harness review workflows where different vendor agents validate each other's outputs to catch bias-driven bugs
source_title: This Meta-Harness Changes How You Run AI Agents

claim: Agents cannot directly read secrets; the meta-harness layer injects them via an approval proxy, making even YOLO mode safer than providing agents direct access
actionable: Never pass secrets to agents directly; use a proxy layer that injects credentials only when tools are called and logs access
source_title: This Meta-Harness Changes How You Run AI Agents

claim: Policies in a meta-harness can depend on history, enabling cost limits, risk scores, repo/file scopes, and PPI scans that are enforced on every tool call
actionable: Design agent policies as stateful guards that examine history before approving tool calls, not as static prompts
source_title: This Meta-Harness Changes How You Run AI Agents

claim: Polly agent orchestrates multiple coding agents in parallel via work trees, routing diffs from different vendors for cross-review before merging
actionable: Use agent orchestrators that spawn isolated work trees per subtask, run in parallel, and feed outputs through cross-vendor validation before convergence
source_title: This Meta-Harness Changes How You Run AI Agents

claim: Multi-harness setups allow users to continue sessions across terminal, web, and mobile interfaces, with all devices viewing the same shared session
actionable: Build or adopt harnesses that persist session state in a central layer so work started in one interface can resume in another
source_title: This Meta-Harness Changes How You Run AI Agents

claim: Agentic AI benefits from being deployed as open-source and battle-tested, with frameworks like OmniGent (Apache 2.0) proving reliability in production
actionable: Evaluate agent frameworks by their maturity and production usage; prioritize open-source solutions with community adoption
source_title: This Meta-Harness Changes How You Run AI Agents
